program: Travel English -- Europe Without a Translator
hub_subtitle: Travel English for the airport, the check-in counter, the hotel and passport control — so that Europe needs no translator

lesson.menu_desc: Answering the officer at the security check, saying what you did not do, and asking him to repeat without panic -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 3 -- Security and the Lounge
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rosângela | Lesson 3 -- Security and the Lounge
lesson.grammar_point: past simple negative and question forms (did not / Did you)

listenings.text: Good morning, and welcome to the security check. Please listen to these instructions before you arrive at the front of the line. First, the rule about liquids. You can only take small bottles: one hundred milliliters or less, and they all go inside one small clear bag. Water, perfume and cream are liquids. If your bottle is bigger than one hundred milliliters, you cannot take it, so please leave it here, in the box on your left. Second, the tray. Take out your laptop, your phone and your keys, and put them in the tray. Your coat and your belt go in the tray too. Then your bag and your tray go through the X-ray machine, and you walk through the metal detector. After the metal detector, take all your things from the tray, and please check that you have your passport and your boarding pass. Then you are free to go to the departure lounge. The duty-free is on your right. Thank you very much, and have a good flight.

Hello. My name is Teresa, and I am waiting for the Lisbon flight, like you. What a morning I had! At the security check, the officer stopped me. Do you know why? I did not remember my perfume. It was a big bottle, one hundred and fifty milliliters, and it was in my carry-on. So it was too big for the liquid restriction, and I lost it. I did not cry, but I was not happy. Then the officer said something very fast, about my coat and the tray, and I did not understand one word. I did not guess, and I did not say yes. I said: I am sorry, I did not catch that. Could you say it more slowly, please? And he did. He repeated it slowly, and he was very kind about it. You know, I did not feel bad. I am sixty-five years old, and I ask people to repeat all the time. Everybody does. And then, after security, I went to the duty-free and I bought another perfume. Why? Because after the security check there is no liquid restriction on what you buy there. You can take it on the plane. So I lost one perfume and I bought a better one. And you? Did you have a good morning?

items.situation: "Good morning. Did you pack this bag yourself?" Answer, and say when.
tips: Good morning. Yes, I did. I packed it yesterday, at home.
My husband packed his bag himself.

items.situation: "Did you bring any liquids in your carry-on?" Your water is at home. Answer, and say why.
tips: No, I did not. I know the liquid restriction.
I did not bring my water. I left it at home.

items.situation: The officer says something very fast and you catch nothing. Do NOT guess. What do you say?
tips: I am sorry, I did not understand. Could you repeat that, please?
I did not catch that. Could you say it more slowly, please?

items.situation: The officer says a word you do not know: "Put it in the tray." Ask what it means.
tips: I am sorry, what does "tray" mean?

items.situation: "Did you leave your bag alone at any moment?" The true answer is no. Use a short answer.
tips: No, I did not. My bag was with me all the time.
No, I did not leave it alone.

items.situation: You are at customs in Lisbon. "Do you have anything to declare?" You have only clothes.
tips: No, I have nothing to declare.
Only my clothes and a present for a friend.

items.situation: The immigration officer asks why you are visiting and how long you are staying.
tips: I am here for tourism, with my husband.
We are staying for fourteen days.

items.situation: You are through security and you do not know where to wait. Ask, and ask about your gate.
tips: Excuse me, where is the departure lounge?
